name: Rio de Janeiro state (Brazil)

colors.symbolism: appears on the central coat of arms
appears on the central coat of arms
appears on the central coat of arms

description: A field divided per saltire into four triangles, with blue at top and bottom and white at hoist and fly; the state's coat of arms is placed at the centre.
history: Adopted in 1975 with the merger of the former state of Guanabara (the city of Rio de Janeiro) and the surrounding state of Rio de Janeiro into a single new state.

features.symbolism: Diagonally divided into four triangles, blue at top and bottom and white at hoist and fly, recalling the unification of the former state of Guanabara with the state of Rio de Janeiro in 1975

features.role: Coat of arms of Rio de Janeiro
features.symbolism: Centred on the field, the state arms display an eagle and the bay of Guanabara among other emblems of the state's history and geography
